metadata.title: Is there a Potential to Apply the Bayesian Approach in Generic Drug Development and Approval? — FDA Workshop Presentation (Carl Peck, UCSF/NDA Partners)
metadata.document_version: FDA Workshop: Leveraging Quantitative Methods and Modeling to Modernize Generic Drug Development and Review — Day 2 AM Session
metadata.extraction_note: IMPORTANT: Despite the filename '20-hepatic-impairment-studies-2020.pdf', this document is NOT an FDA guidance on hepatic impairment studies. It is a 10-slide conference presentation (PowerPoint-style slides) from the FDA Workshop on Leveraging Quantitative Methods and Modeling to Modernize Generic Drug Development and Review, October 3, 2017, Washington DC. The presenter is Carl Peck (UCSF, NDA Partners). The content is a provocation/discussion piece on whether Bayesian approaches could be applied to generic drug development and approval. It contains no regulatory requirements, no mandatory rules, no study design specifications, and no submission requirements. It is a historical overview and speculative discussion of statistical methodology. No regulatory rules can be legitimately extracted from this document without fabrication. Zero rules are extracted in compliance with the quality requirement that every rule must be traceable to specific document text establishing a regulatory requirement.
